competitors.name: momentum_breakout
competitors.rules: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.

competitors.name: blended_momo_rsi
competitors.rules: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.

competitors.name: mean_reversion
competitors.rules: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.

competitors.name: mirofish_real
competitors.rules: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds; consensus picks (weight ∝ vote share) rebalanced each tick.

competitors.name: deep_research_analyst
competitors.rules: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).

competitors.name: llm_voters
competitors.rules: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.

analyst.as_of: 2026-06-21 (Sunday; daily bars through 2026-06-18 close — Fri 6/19 was the Juneteenth market holiday)
analyst.sizing: One-week-upside mandate: 4 momentum/catalyst names, only ~15% cash, semis-led around MU's 6/24 print
analyst.framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen → /sector → /comps → /catalysts → /thesis → portfolio). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).

regime.label: Hawkish-pause Fed, but a tech-led tape into a marquee semis catalyst week
regime.note: New Chair Kevin Warsh's first FOMC was a hawkish pause — held 3.5–3.75% and removed the 2026 cut from the dots, with half of officials now penciling a hike — yet last week was tech-led (Nasdaq +2.4%) and the weekly forecast is 'moderately bullish' with bumpier tech action. The defining one-week event is Micron's 6/24 print, which sets the tone for the whole memory/AI-semis complex; the macro swing factor is the midweek PCE inflation read (the Fed's preferred gauge). This tick's explicit mandate is one-week upside, so the book leans into momentum leadership and keeps only a light cash hedge for the PCE/Fed tail.

analyst.thesis: Mandate this tick: what rises THIS week (Mon 6/22–Fri 6/26). The screen says one thing — semis lead and everything else lags. The week's center of gravity is Micron, which reports fiscal Q3 after the close on Wednesday 6/24 (consensus ~$35B revenue, +276% YoY, ~$20 EPS) amid a sold-out-HBM memory super-cycle; MU is already a momentum leader (+14% 5d, +55% 20d) at a fresh 52-wk high, and a strong print is the most likely single catalyst to lift the whole sleeve into week-end. The book is built around that: MU is the anchor (30%, sized for a binary print, not a coin-flip); ARM (20%) is the strongest momentum in the complex (+28% 5d, +71% 20d, fresh high) and the highest-beta IP name to a good MU read; SOXL (20%→15%) is the 3x-semis expression that amplifies a leadership week, consistent with the high-variance mandate. HOOD (20%) is the deliberate non-semi diversifier — fintech/crypto beta, +43% 20d, yet still ~30% below its Oct high (room to run) and with no binary event this week. Unlike last tick's 'refuse the parabolas' stance, the one-week-upside focus accepts ARM's rich multiple and SOXL's leverage as the price of momentum; the trade-off is a thinner ~15% cash hedge for the midweek PCE print.

evidence.point: Micron reports fiscal Q3 after close Wed 6/24; consensus ~$35B revenue (+276% YoY), ~$20 EPS, memory/HBM super-cycle — the marquee single-name catalyst of the week
MU 53.5x trailing P/E but at a fresh 52-wk high ($103→$1,149 in 10 months, last $1,134); record guide collapses the forward multiple — priced for perfection into the print
ARM +28% 5d / +71% 20d at a fresh 52-wk high — strongest momentum in the semis complex and high-beta to a good MU read (rich at 519x P/E, the key risk)
HOOD +43% 20d, PE 52, still ~30% below its Oct-2025 high ($154) — momentum with room and no binary event this week; non-semi diversifier
Fed (Warsh) hawkish pause — held 3.5–3.75%, removed 2026 cut; week is 'moderately bullish' but midweek PCE is the macro swing factor
Earnings week 6/22–6/26: MU (6/24) the standout; Nike reports 6/30 (just after) — little else in-universe carries a this-week catalyst

risks: MU's 6/24 after-close print is binary and the move lands 6/25 — a soft HBM/guidance read can gap the whole memory complex (MU+ARM+SOXL = 65% of the book) down together; the semi concentration is the bet and the risk.
Everything in this book is at or near a 52-wk high; a hot midweek PCE / hawkish higher-for-longer reaction does the most damage to exactly these extended, long-duration names.
Leaning into ARM (519x P/E) and 3x SOXL is the opposite of last tick's stance — if the parabola breaks rather than extends, the lighter ~15% cash hedge cushions less.
SOXL is a 3x leveraged ETF — path-dependent decay punishes a choppy, sideways week even if semis finish flat.

data_examined.label: Screen: 5d/10d/21d/63d/126d momentum, vol-surge, 20d-SMA distance, 6-mo range-position across the 100-name universe
Comps: P/E, P/B, market cap, 52-wk range, avg volume on MU/ARM/HOOD/AMD/NVDA/MRVL
Regime + catalysts: hawkish-pause Fed, midweek PCE, MU 6/24 earnings, week-of-6/22 earnings calendar

analyst.generated_by: Claude for Financial Services (equity-research plugin methodology) via Claude Code, 2026-06-21 — one-week-upside focus
analyst.note: Daily bars run through the 2026-06-18 close (6/19 Juneteenth holiday; weekend 6/20–6/21). Fundamentals live as of 2026-06-18. This tick's mandate is explicitly one-week upside, so the book is more momentum/catalyst-tilted and lower-cash than a balanced allocation. Sets the analyst's next-tick targets; the competitor books advance on the next completed session.

reflection.looking_back: Last tick I rotated the whole book — exited JPM, the old NVDA lot, GE and QCOM and rebuilt around semis momentum (MU/ARM/SOXL/HOOD). The exits realized roughly flat (JPM -0.4%, NVDA fractionally green), so the rotation cost almost nothing. The new positions were entered 6/18 and have barely moved since — the thesis is still unproven and now rides almost entirely on Micron's 6/24 print.
worked: Cutting JPM and the stale NVDA lot before rotating — both exits were near-flat, so re-deploying into momentum leaders cost essentially zero P&L.
Flagging MU's 6/24 print as the week's center of gravity and making it the sized anchor rather than a coin-flip add.
missed: Correlation, not just position size: MU+ARM+SOXL is ~65% of the book on one binary catalyst — a soft HBM read gaps all three together, a concentration the prior sizing under-weighted.
Entered at/near 52-wk highs with only a ~15% cash hedge — thin dry powder if the midweek PCE print runs hot.
reflection.adjustment: Hold the semis thesis into the 6/24 print but treat MU/ARM/SOXL as one correlated bet — if MU guides soft, trim the three together rather than as independent names.

swarm.note: Live swarm — 150 fish, each a unique trader profile shown its own random ~20-name slice of the watchlist (with recent headlines), voted via OpenRouter. Independent-voter election; dissenters (ringed) chose CASH.

live.note: Connected to the Agentic cash account •••• — currently unfunded ($0). Deposit into this specific account to trade the real $100. No agent orders placed yet.

rules.momentum_breakout: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.
rules.mean_reversion: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.
rules.blended_momo_rsi: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.
rules.deep_research_analyst: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).
rules.llm_voters: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.
rules.mirofish_real: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds; consensus picks (weight ∝ vote share) rebalanced each tick.

sources.name: Standings, curves, decision trail (LIVE)
sources.detail: The forward paper test — every competitor trades a $100 book the same way, advanced one trading day per tick (rule strategies via tick.py / bot/engine.py; agents via run_agents.py / bot/paper.py), 5 bps slippage, −15% stop. Young — it grows one session per tick.

sources.name: Backtest reference
sources.detail: A separate Dec–Jun walk-forward backtest (2025-12-01–2026-06-18, in-sample, mostly-bull) is shown inside each rule strategy's detail for context only — it is NOT the live board.

sources.name: Research analyst
sources.detail: Agent-driven on the Claude Code plan: web_search + Robinhood data → one report (state/analyst.json) with target weights per tick. Sources link from each evidence row.

sources.name: Swarm vote
sources.detail: Independent-voter election: 150 unique-profile cheap models (DeepSeek/Gemini/Llama/Haiku via OpenRouter) each read one shared briefing and return a single ballot; tallied in bot/swarm.py. No interaction between fish.

sources.name: Real-MiroFish swarm
sources.detail: Social-simulation swarm (bot/mirofish.py): persona agents with memory interact over multiple rounds — each re-votes seeing its own prior view and its neighbours' latest — so a consensus forms via peer influence (the opposite of the independent swarm). Tiered cost (cheap/default/qwen); seeded from the briefing + optional world-events news.

sources.name: Live account
sources.detail: Robinhood MCP, Agentic cash account ••••, via get_portfolio / get_equity_positions / get_equity_orders.

sources.name: Display scale
sources.detail: The real paper books are $100 each; every dollar and share figure on this site is shown ×100 (a $10,000 book) so holdings read in whole shares and dollars. Per-share prices and percentages are unscaled.
